reviews.review_text:
[h1] A great, but flawed fast paced, action-focused CO-OP experience. It's still worth playing with a friend. 7.5/10 [/h1]

Resident Evil 5 is a fun, story driven 2 player co-op game with an emphasis on action instead of horror. It utilizes Resident Evil 4's mechanical blueprint for gameplay and it's great, but there's a few negatives that drags it down.

Gameplay Pros: The gunplay is solid, offering a great variety of options to choose from each weapon class. Nearly every gun is viable and fairly balanced when compared to one another. You can also upgrade all of your weapons in the shop using the gold dropped by enemies and gains earned from selling the collectible treasures. The enemies aren't bullet spongey and they constantly drop loot when you defeat them so resources aren't very scarce. The main game features 6 chapters and the levels are paced well. There are also additional multiplayer game-modes you can experience after the main story.

Gameplay Cons: All of the boss fights and mini-boss fights are [b] GARBAGE!! [/b] Del Lago and El Gigante basically return from RE4 and you fight these bosses during turret sections. You're just holding M1 and occasionally dodging whenever the prompt to do so comes up. For other bosses, you're either blasting their glowing weak points or defeating them with some scripted QTE button mashing. The 5-2 boss is [b] BROKEN [/b] on this port of the game, and CANNOT be killed using the flamethrower the game gives you. I ended up pulling out an RPG and obliterated it after wasting over 10+ minutes. You can one shot most bosses in the game with the RPG which is great for speed-runs. The boss fights are a big downgrade from RE4 which was disappointing.

Another issue with this game is the inventory. You only have 9 slots and that's all you get. I carried 3 guns, ammo for those guns, and healing or utility items for the last 3 slots. You will run into a lot of discarding or trading situations with your friend which can hinder the action. This is a big issue early game. Later in future playthroughs, you can unlock and purchase guns with bottomless mags, so you won't have to carry ammo anymore which is great.

Story: Save the world by stopping Wesker's doomsday plan, he also aura farms a lot during combat and cutscenes. He 100% carries this game and is probably the best villain in RE. The other two villains you defeat before getting to Wesker are stereotypical, and the narrative treats them like fodder trash.

TL;DR Great action-focused, CO-OP game that is 100% worth playing with a friend. The boss fights are trash and the inventory system is small, but the game makes up for these shortcomings with late game unlocks, great main villain, and the amount of fun and laughs you will experience.

Resident Evil 5 ended up being the best game I've played all year, largely because of how it completely transformed from what was supposed to be survival horror, into a co-op comedy experience from start to finish. I genuinely spent more time laughing at this game than playing it—not at any one thing in particular, but at the constant accumulation of absurd moments that kept building on each other.

A big part of that came down to my friend's outfit. I don't even think I can properly explain it, but the fact they insisted on running around in [i]that[/i] look made every single cutscene and encounter infinitely funnier. No matter how serious the game tried to be, the tone was instantly undercut the moment she appeared on screen, and it became impossible to take anything seriously after. And then there were the moments that completely broke us both: Me deciding to bring a sniper rifle into a cave and immediately becoming useless. My friend constantly reframing everything mid-cutscene—like "Chris' now facing a tough decision, between blondie and brunette", Wesker turning into a running joke as Zeno, the overly dramatic "I have a very long gun because I'm compensating for something" antagonist, especially funny because of his attempted cosplay of Neo from the Matrix.

Heck, even the most basic of interactions turned into pure comedy—I opened a door straight into an enemy holding an RPG, panicked, sprinted into him, and the rocket detonated instantly—less "take a bullet for you", more "take a rocket for you"? xD And the "Vulcan Raven" minigun boss fights had us in stitches, TWICE! Chris uppercutting a literal ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ boulder? Ahaha, it will never stop being funny no matter how many times I think about it.

That energy carried through everything—set pieces, boss fights, even basic traversal mechanics. There were so many moments where I genuinely had to put the controller down because I was laughing too much to continue playing. This game just kept producing ridiculous situations, and then there was the final sequence on the plane... I don't even know what happened to my composure at that point. Between the chaos of the scene itself and my friend still in [i]that[/i] outfit, I was laughing so much I could barely watch the rest of the cutscene.

It completely sealed the experience for me—co-op chaos in its funniest form; Resident Evil 5, easily my favourite gaming memory of the year!

I opened Resident Evil 5 expecting bad controls. Because a lot of people were hating on the stop and shoot mechanics. tbh i don't hate it at all. it actually gives the game a unique experience combined with the "piss filter" it really gave me a nostalgic feeling for the Xbox 360 days.

I played the game with one of my friends and it was really chaotic in the best way possible. We were arguing over materials, rushing for every ammo pack on the ground and spamming emotes nonstop.
And every time Wesker showed up we got hyped especially when he said "7 minutes" that was so peak.
If you haven't played Resident Evil 5 yet, i genuinely think you should. it's a really unique experience.

I wasn't sure if I would enjoy 5 all that much just from some initial impressions seeing it beforehand online over the years, but after finally getting around to it, man it hooked me immediately. It feels like a sort of cheesy 2000s action film mixed with some familiar resident evil-isms as a nice cherry on top. The story was also pretty interesting and reading through the documents found in chapters was a good time. I would say the part I enjoyed most was playing it solo and juggling the gameplay between two characters as well, besides the couple times the teammate AI would function with 2 braincells, it was pretty fun. Certain parts reminded me of of my favorite campaign in l4d2 (Hard Rain) for some reason as well,, so can't complain there. 

Not sure if an unpopular opinion or not though, but I personally hated the majority of the boss fights. Something about them were just not very fun to me, but other than that I enjoyed the game for what it is and am glad to have chugged through it.

This is just pure co-op fun with lots of meme value.

It has really cool balance between beat'em up and shooting, levels with surprise mechanics for both players to enjoy, lots of additional game modes and missions along with questionable outfits for the characters...

Unfortunately, you can't play any online game modes unless you have 4 friends but I heard that you could play those with bots through mods.

I didn't expect much from this game and kinda wanted to rush through it to get to RE6 but it pleasantly surprised me so much that I replayed it solo after beating it with my friend.

People say how bad the bot companion is but it's not. It does so many things right that it's pretty rare for them to get stuck or die randomly, although there are a few segments that were not playtested well.

And be sure to disable "attack reaction" because your character shoots from the center of the screen and, because your companion most of the time behind you, your weapon will magically shoot them, this messed up lots of the shots during boss fights.

Speaking of boss fights: I love how all of them have some kind of gimmick, it's like a puzzle that you have to understand to beat, sometimes just shooting the thing is not enough.

I remember the hate for this game. Now, ironically, it is more faithful to RE4 than RE4 Remake.

Tank controls feel great and add tension to gameplay, it doesn't feel bad to use and didn't have to be modernized (a.k.a. removed completely in every future game).

Anyway, RE5 is much more fun than I expected because all this time, in my mind, it was just one of those late 2000 shooters with piss filter.
Best multiplayer games ever made, just need a remake version after veronica remake xD
Very fun for a lil co-op experience. Only struggled with a bug with the boss on Chapter 5-2, where you should manually cap your FPS to 30 FPS. But that's it, really fun experience.

this is genuinely the best game ever in every possible way, not even exaggerating 🙏 i replay this game every 3 months cause of how good it is

i need a remake NOWWWWWWW
Its a great game a solid 8 out of 10, its better with mods as u can have more fun with the game

This game is not very good. The story at the start of the game is incredibly poor as its hard to understand and confusing. This game has no real interesting features that separates it from other games. Wouldn't recommend this to anyone, even if its free

Message @John Pork

played half of the game with some random italian dude from soc who would never speak or laugh at any of my jokes. he unfriended me on discord one day when we were half way through the game. no explanation. i didn't get to finish it but i remember that it was pretty good when i played it as a kid at least.

A solid good game honestly. Resident Evil 5 is over-hated in my opinion. It should get more love.

Though the 2 things I didn't like is...
1, You can't reload while moving.
2. You are have to always use the scope on the sniper rifles to fire them. I wish the game had the option to choice between using the scope or not.

RE5 is just nonstop escalation. Starts off as two dudes shooting zombies in Africa and somehow ends up with laser rooms, giant monsters, and boat chases while the game throws every ridiculous idea it has at you.
Chris and Sheva carried the most braindead gameplay decisions known to man and still made it entertaining. Pure action movie nonsense.
10/10 would go on another terrible tropical vacation again
game doesn't even work anymore. crashes on start up. tried everything to fix it. maybe one day they will remaster it or something.

Very ordinary type game. Feels like a game older than it's age in terms of build quality. Boring story. Boring characters, Chris Redfield is just a nothing military type guy. Nothing combat. Feels like it could have originally been a light gun game.

I will praise it for having the first good boss fight in a Resi game (good boss fight is rare in video games in general) with the first Wesker fight, RE3's final fight was alright but this one was stand out GOOD. It then loses all that with the final fight which had two separate soft locking issues that were not clear they were issues.

Was really hoping Excella would turn into a huge monster with huge boobs, but alas.

4 / 10

This isn't to say the experience was a 4/10 bad, I had a fun time with my friend! But in a Mystery Science Theatre sorta way.

whats the story even about??? the camera movements gave me motion sickness and its just action based game and no horror like watafac??? the best part are chris' milky breasts, sheva outfit and the hand placement on albert wesker.

I love RE games, but there are a few things in this one that I cannot stand and it's a shame because overall it's not that bad.

1) Keyboard and mouse are badly optimized, really strange to move.

2) It's 100% an action shooting game, not a survival horror (which is not bad per se, but I find myself uninterested in the story because all you do is shoot).

3) I guess if you are playing with a friend is fun. But as a single player experience sometimes is quite difficult because your bot-partner is not that bright.

4) Finally, one stupid impossible long boss fight ruined the game for me. Cannot and will not try to progress anymore, for me this was the last straw.

Even though it's practically a reskinned Resident Evil 4 in co-op with massive enemy spam, it's actually pretty fun and definitely super over the top but in a good way. If you've got someone to play with there's basically no reason not to give it a shot.
I am biased because this was the first ever RE game I really played, so it is my favorite. Cant wait for a future remake.

The most terrifying part about this game isn't the enemies, it isn't the environement, it isn't the music.
The MOST terrifying part about this game is picking up keys.

My friend convinced me to play it. I was skeptical because I hadn't heard many good things about the game. OH BOY, when I tell you we played till 5 in the morning and we stopped because I had university exam in a few hours. The game controls suck but you get used to them and stop paying attention. 
Our attention went to make fun of Chris shirt, bro how did you even put it on???? 
I recommend to play it with controller. Even tho my friend played it from his  keyboard. 
I searched for a nice co op, with good story and to be fun...
DIDNT THINK IT WILL BE UNDER MY NOSE IN MY FAVOURITE GAME SERIES 
(we will try re6 as well even tho I have heard worse things about it)
It's a night and day difference playing with an actual person versus playing with the ai. Highly recommended to play with the former.

I think this game is pretty fun but I’d say realistically only play this if you love the original resident evil 4 and want more of the same combat but everything else to be more annoying.
You have to play with a partner and the ai is not terrible if you play solo but it feels like a lot of the late game is trying to force you to play online by putting you in situations where both people need to know what they’re doing.
The final boss fight was a rube Goldberg piece of nonsense that dragged on forever. 
And obviously unless you’re pretty insensitive, you’re going to be troubled by all the scenes where you’re killing African tribes and stealing their artifacts.
It’s just not a great game but it might be fun if you played re4 and are craving more of it
The extremely bad camera movement and a lot of screen shake make it unenjoyable, even when not taking into consideration the green filter and small FOV.
very good action co-op game to play, though its still fun fpr single-player but you will missed the the advantage of this co-op feature.

this game is genuinely so much fun with a friend. it was weird playing at first, controls were very.. questionable. i won't even get started on the horrible camera. other than that, solid game, solid story. 

though it felt like a weird homosexual romcom between chris and the 7 minutes guy.
Both Sheva and the trademarked piss filter are hot, this is my favorite RE game of all time.

The mouse settings alone make this game borderline unplayable. It doesn't move at the same speed vertically and horizontally, so that no matter what your mouse sensitivity is at, it'll be quite insufferable to aim or move. You can either aim well, but turn around at a snails pace, or be able to turn normally but your aim is incredibly sensitive. This issue alone compounds every other issue I had with the gameplay, as it just made the overall experience feel janky and poorly optimised. Perhaps it was not meant to be played on keyboard and mouse, but then maybe it should not be released on a computer.

The combat brings me no joy. When aiming the gun, you cannot move, so just have to stop and start anytime you want to adjust positioning. Typically standing still is okay since although the zombies charge at you, they like to stop just short of attacking you for some reason, making the combat not that threatening. The main issue I have is with the lack of ammo, in tandem with the general bulkiness of each zombie as well as the sheer amount of zombies. They just spam infinite waves, which is fine, they're decently bulky unless you hit a nice head shot which is admittedly satisfying, but also you are sparsely granted ammo. You are also expected to share ammo with your teammate, which is a problem since I run out of ammo just by myself, so she usually just stands around and gets in the way. 

Ok so the ammo is a problem, luckily you have a knife. When wielding the knife, you for some reason can also not move. This would be okay if the knife also didn't have slight knock back, meaning you can hit a zombie a couple times before it is out of range, so you have to do a janky stop and start to hit them again. In general the combat does not feel smooth or rewarding in any way, aside from the one-shot head shots, which are also not that consistent.

In the sections I played, for the most part it was quite straight forward. Just follow the path. In some sections, you just get hit with nonstop waves of zombies and have to run around for an arbitrary amount of time until the plot decides you're done in that section. There's no timer or any indication of progress. You don't even have to kill the big guy walking around. It just ends at some point, which makes it feel like I didn't really achieve anything other than waste time and ammo.

Obviously I did not play through much of the game, I just wanted to try it out because I heard resident evil is good and it was on sale, but I was overall disappointed. I'm sure at some point the game picked up and felt more satisfying, but I could not force myself to slog through this in the hopes it got better. Nothing in this game made me interested to continue playing, and so that is where I will leave it for the foreseeable future.

This Game is alot of fun. This game is really fun Co-op. I enjoy it alot the story is a bit over the top. But the graphics still hold up and the gameplay is extremely fun. I highly recommend this game.

I love this franchise. But the people who made this game did not understand what made RE4 good. The removal of save locations and fixed merchant locations completely removes the tension of being alone and left to your own devices. The greater frequency of enemies and the co-op mechanic just compounds this. It's still fun, and god knows they've sold a lot of copies, but this game is such a missed opportunity.
Game is 8/10,good combos,good story,the camera angle is a bit hard,but a lot of new enemies and bosses !
This game is a set with the song "ATC - Around the World." Immerse yourself in memories.

Las horas de diversión en multijugador te tiene que dar en una review un juegazo. Jugar en singleplayer debe ser una de las experiencias más pesadas de un Resident Evil, y no es quedarse corto sobretodo por la cantidad de Logros para los DLC/Multijugador.
Fun game and is made to be played in Co-op, so play with a friend!  Works well on Linux.
Seven minutes. Seven minutes is all I can spare to play with you. Here I am offering you my precious time, and you waste it by running around.

Very active still, pretty fun disabled the QTE in this version it seems, decently fun still to play would recommend when on sale for $5 did two runs one solo, and one where i had a co op partners join( Randoms) downside is not being able to see co op profile so when game ends you prolly wont see them again. 7/10
resident evil 4 and africa and your partner isn't a useless potato
Beautiful game with massive re-playability and customization of weapons. Killer co-op too.
game is good, but the boss fights are ♥♥♥♥ and the boss ai and logic is broken

I decided to start the Resident Evil series after seeing the Albert Wesker meme. It hyped me up, and I really wanted to get to this game quickly. Finally, when it was this game's turn, I played it with pleasure. Compared to the first games, the game has turned more into an action game. There are almost no puzzles to speak of. I think its graphics are insufficient for that period, but it still holds up. The game engine is not bad, and the scenes with Albert Wesker are great. It continues the series beautifully.

Lo jugue en dificil en modo lobo solitario junto a la Sheva (IA) y vaya estres me pegue en varias partes. Tambien es complicado encontrar algo que este juego haga siquiera al mismo nivel que el 4. El inventario esta muy limitado y su funcionamiento es raro e incomodo en medio del combate si usas a Sheva como contenedor secundario para tener a disposicion una variedad decente de armas. Muchos enemigos se sienten desinspirados o directamente un copia y pega del anterior juego. Y los escenarios son menos interesantes. 
Realmente estaba contando los capitulos por ver cuando terminaba mi suplicio, y aunque para mi no sea un ¨aprobado¨, si que lo recomiendo. Tiene sus momentos y estoy seguro que con un amigo es divertidisimo. Pienso que es una buena parada para conocer mas de la historia de la saga.

Quite possibly one of the worst games I have ever played. Every enemy is a bullet sponge. They decided to make a horde shooter, but stayed with the tank controls from RE4. Everything comes in hordes, and everything has more health than you have bullets in the magazine. The AI is awful. Why isn't Sheva doing anything? There are three enemies within poking distance, AND YOU HAVE A FULLY LOADED SHOTGUN SHEVA, SHOOT THE ENEMIES SHEVA

An absolute INSULT to the Resident Evil name. There are no puzzles (I refuse to count the mirrors, it is one of the easiest puzzles in ANY RE game I've played.), no exploration (The Marsh is the closest this gets, but there is only what you strictly need to progress. There's no optional areas to get extra stuff.). There is no reason to go out of your way, even if it was really an option.

Oh man, but at least there's enemy variety-oh wait, there are like three enemies in the whole game and they're all just copy and pastes from RE4, except black and with African weapons?

Well at least RE5 has good bosses like the games before it! Oh wait, so many bosses are copy and pasted, and are terrible to fight against? The second Uroburos (I don't know if I'm spelling that right, but I don't have the respect for this game to spell it right,) is one of the worst boss fights in any game I've ever played. I love spending thirty minutes on one boss. Isn't that fun? It's so fun right?

There's no reason for the co-op other than a selling point. The game has to justify itself at any chance it gets by separating Chris and Sheva, and these sections are without fail THE WORST PARTS OF THE GAME.

All of the characters are one note, and nothing, even for a Resident Evil game. They practically try to lampshade that RE5 is just RE4 but Africa, but it's just, "What if RE4, but instead of all of those good parts of RE4, we took out, and instead you get co-op!" It's quite literally just Las Plagas, but now everything is brown and dull shades of orange. 

This game is impossibly awful. Offensively awful.

Maybe if this game gets re-made it'll be good. I hope it does, because then there will be zero reason to buy this god awful piece of ♥♥♥♥.

Honestly i find this game enjoyable despite the quirks, it's a good  casual experience solo on easy mode or play with a friend for a fun and chaotic experience.
The graphics are not too bad if you consider how old the game is and it runs very smoothly on a decent PC
a very enjoyable game with friends, very entertaining combat.

It's a pretty decent Resident Evil game, the story is extremely cool but good golly I'd never have played this if it weren't a Resident Evil game. The controls just make it so frustrating and yes, I got used to them by chapter 2/3 but it doesn't change the fact that tank controls HAD TO GO.

The game is held back by its atrocious filter and gameplay mechanics of its time but other than that it's an ok game, its merit lies in the huge amount of lore it contained, tying everything up from past and present. It's kinda goofy and fun at the same time. Plus, the DLCs were pretty good as well. 

It's like a very B-side action movie with a very (very) slight horror backstory. It's fun.

kind of confused as to how this game exists in the state that it does, admittedly its been a while since i've played the other originals, but i seem to recall them playing much better than this

a lot of people point out that this game leans too much into action and not enough into survival horror, and i think thats a fair analysis, it feels like a collection of mediocre boss fights strung together with an interesting setting, and a few wave-based combat sections in between. however, despite not really having any horror elements present, it still feels distinctly resident evil in how it plays

the bigger issue in my opinion is how poorly executed it all feels

despite releasing on pc, this doesn't feel like something designed with one in mind. the sensitivity is an absolute nightmare, somehow it manages to be both too slow and too fast simultaneously, depending on the action you are trying to do, and there's no way to fine tune one without making the other feel even worse

the boss fights seem to have one-maybe-two stages designed for them each, and in a lot of cases they will repeat those stages 3-5 times with no clear sign of progression until suddenly the fight is over. in the event of a fight with a giant crab creature, there is a specific action you are expected to take to progress, and if you don't do it / don't do it in the way the game specifically wants, you will just cycle through endlessly (i burned around 20 minutes doing this before finally figuring it out by accidentally stumbling upon a hidden button prompt)

and a lot of the combat encounters just feel bad, about an hour ago i went through a hallway with like 20 lickers that felt designed purely to eat up all the extra ammunition i gained in the section prior, and it just felt lame! had it been skipped and a few extra enemies been placed in the room following, the overall experience would have been improved


despite this, i do think the game offers a good experience for the most part, and there's something to be said about the RE charm that keeps drawing me back into this, it all just kind of falls into place with a compelling enough story and enough little environmental puzzles to make it feel worth pushing through some of the jank

it is also becoming incredibly apparent to me the more i play that this game in particular had a strong impact on how The Evil Within series came into existence - TEW2 is one of my favourite survival horror releases and i belive it shares a lot of DNA with RE5



all that was written before i started chapter 5 so here's a couple extra thoughts
- wow yeah they really built this with a few very specific ways to play in mind, any deviation from that and u will be doomed in a billion ways in these boss fights
- oh yea i just killed wesker with a knife because i ran out of ammo
- why the hell did i have hardly any ammo left by the final fight
- oh yeah i forgot the boulder punch is in this
- the weird insect things added nothing to the experience
- resale value on ur items is really bad and this messed me up a lot
- theres a boss fight involving a flamethrower, i struggled to beat this for a while because playing how i thought it was supposed to, didn't work. upon looking up tips for the fight i learned that actually i was playing it right, they just broke it in this version of the game, but on the original releases what i was doing wouldve been fine
- inventory management in this is truly miserable


overall the game isnt as bad as people make it out to be, but it does have a lot of fundamental flaws in its design. it still feels distinctly resident evil, and i enjoyed my time with it. bring on re6!

[h1] SEVEN MINUTES. SEVEN MINUTES IS ALL I CAN SPARE TO PLAY RESIDENT EVIL 5 [/h1]


[b]Resident Evil 5[/b] is one of those games I had been hyped for forever but never actually got the chance to experience until now. A huge reason for that was Albert Wesker. Seeing one of the series’ most iconic characters make his grand return here immediately made this game feel important to me, especially after learning more about the lore from the previous titles.

Lately, I’ve basically been having a full “Resident Evil month,” sitting down and playing through the series one game after another nonstop. Sometimes I even managed to finish some of the shorter titles within a single day. Going through the franchise this way has honestly been a really fun experience because you start noticing how everything connects together over time.

That being said, I still ran into the same issue I had with several of the older [b]Resident Evil[/b]  games - the tank controls and outdated gameplay mechanics. I understand these games were revolutionary back in the day, but by modern standards some of the movement and combat systems can feel clunky and difficult to fully enjoy, at least for me personally.

What really kept me invested was the lore. I loved learning more about the origins of the T-Virus and how everything traces back to the Progenitor Virus and the African flower. Seeing how human greed, corruption, and obsession twisted scientific discoveries into biological weapons was genuinely interesting. The [b]Resident Evil[/b] universe always does a great job showing how different people keep creating their own variations of these viruses in pursuit of power and control - and somehow everything eventually turns into another horrifying giant mutation you have to put down by the end.

What also makes Wesker such an entertaining villain is how he constantly feels like he’s behind everything in the [b]Resident Evil[/b] universe. The man is basically collecting viruses like Infinity Stones, always chasing the next ultimate lifeform or world-ending weapon while secretly plotting something in the background.
Despite some outdated mechanics, I still had a really good time with [b]RE5[/b]. The action, the co-op focus, the story connections, and especially Wesker’s presence made the experience memorable. And honestly, after seeing what Capcom achieved with the recent remakes, this is another title that feels like it has massive potential for a modern reimagining.

I know I’ve mentioned remakes many times throughout my [b]Resident Evil[/b] reviews, but I genuinely believe this is the best way for newer players to experience these stories. The originals already have something special at their core - the remakes simply help bring that potential fully to life!
And in the end, Mr. Wesker really gave us seven minutes of pure suffering just to leave afterward and call us “disappointing” like we ruined his evening... :((

i enjoy the combat movement and QTE in this one kinda funny but the problem inventory system is too few with lot of stuff this game offer, probably coz of the coop feature which how this game should be played with other to have more fun experience, anyway after finished the campaign its still solid 8/10 experience for OG game

Great Game , don't go in it thinking of it as a horror game, but a combination of both horror and action, great for people who are fan of the series too, a lot of character stuff happens but you don't need to play older games to understand it. 

CHRIS IS THE GOAT......

Good fun.
A little janky, a little bit dated, but still fun as hell, and with an engaging story and badass characters.
Of all Resident Evil games, this was the first where playing it felt frustrating - mostly due to bad keyboard & mouse controls and 0 IQ AI, unevenly paced and poorly balanced enemies and gunplay.
It's cool, obviously not better than the remakes but it's still a good game.
i love 5, its not better than 4 but i see it as the most fun action based RE game with the perfect co-op in any resident evil game unless you count 6 which yeah has co-op but its not as good as 5.
Played when it originally released still Fun to this day better enjoyed as co op in my opinion.

This game is a 10/10 if you're looking for one of the best co-op experiences ever made, and for a game released in 2009, it still holds up supremely well. 

However if you're an achievement hunter like myself, the multiplayer achievements are a pain in the <3<3<3 to get. So there's that. Game has been a journey and glad to be done with it (at least for now).

i feel mixed about this game but i feel like overall i did not enjoy it

the reason i didn't enjoy it is because how much of it is forced co op, you can play single player (in which i did for the entire game) but its a pain in the ass since the main game is not built at all for singe player you get a new god awful inventory system that is a pain in the ass with your partner, and you also have to share ammo and enemies have a ton of health even on normal mode 
and with the partner system it doesn't feel scary at all anymore since there is no loneliness no real fear to be had there are occasional monsters that i do think have scary designs but i cannot be scared with someone at my side that helps me

but what i love are the cut scenes and some of the story, it really makes no sense at the beginning but the new villains it introduces and wesker dramatically improve the game i cannot state how much i love this wesker he is greatly elevated compared to his first appearance 

but i feel like the forced co op for a series that has been single player for decades is the worst option and is what made me not enjoy this game all together

More of a neutral rating than straight recommendation I suppose. An obvious step down in overall quality from Resi 4, although it still looks quite good, especially in cutscenes. The cinematography wears its influence on its sleeve and the performance capture (if that's what they used) still looks decent. The level design shows its age, its a game from that era where the textures and overall clutter detail may have increased, the level design and geometry still has that Gamecube/ PS2 quality to it. The tension of RE4 is pretty much totally gone, but that's not necessarily a bad thing. This game is going for a more bombastic tone, and it hits that where the set pieces are a lot of waves of enemies usually, rather than skulking through an environment. The game truly shines in co op though, as the AI for Sheva is pretty stupid. I got a ton of game overs in the final boss just because she'd get herself killed or just didn't want to shoot the enemy for some reason. The story is mostly okay, although there's not a lot there. It's quite front and back loaded, the middle involves the plot and gameplay just kinda spinning its wheels for 2 to 3 chapters. Its pretty silly, although not as much as what comes after this...
The only other major complaint that comes to mind is the music. During combat it has this drone that plays in some places whenever an enemy is hunting you down. It can get pretty grating and it stopping the second you kill the last enemy can be a bit jarring. I suppose this was how they tried to keep that horror tension through the music, but it ends up just feeling like you have some bees hovering near you or something. 
The mercenaries is a fun game mode, and probably justifies this game's existence on its own to be honest. 
But yeah otherwise this is enjoyable and a relatively quick romp. A note for people playing though: the boss at the end of chapter 5-2 is bugged if you play over 60fps, if you don't lock the game to 60fps in the settings it will just be invincible to the method you're supposed to kill it with and alternate methods will give close the damage window far too quickly.

im not gonna write an all out review like usual, this comes from an coop experience, this game had good moments, the dlc is okay-ish,  both dlcs are only about 30 min worth of gameplay. overall its kinda mid and you wont miss out on much if you just watch an summary

[h1]Resident Evil 5[/h1]

Easy 4/10 experience.

Story is fine but this has been by far the worst game I have played this year so far as I'm playing through all the mainline RE games this year my first time. While this game resembles the OG RE4 gameplay which I do greatly appreciate and have fun in that aspect, the whole forced co-op experience really weighs down this game with it's God awful inventory management and dumb AI companion on top of it. The game lacks horror although I'm okay with it, it's a RE game but this is definitely the sign of time when RE was really starting it's downfall.

While you can play this game solo, it's not fun, by the end of the game I was just dreading for the game to end and be done with it. I don't recommend unless you really want an 'okay' experience of a co-op game with a friend. I don't see myself playing this again honestly and glad it's over.

Probably an unpopular opinion but RE5 and RE6 are my favorite Resident Evil games. I just like how they focus more on action and combat. It's fun to just play through the campaign, especially in co-op. The movement controls are a bit clunky and took some getting used too, but otherwise the game is great. I'd definitely recommend it.

Resident Evil 5 is a third-person shooter about Chris and Sheva in a fictional region of West Africa. Chris learns that he must confront his past in the form of an old enemy, Albert Wesker, and his former partner, Jill Valentine. I’m excited to talk about this one because this is actually my full introduction to the Resident Evil franchise. I know, very strange for the 6th game of the main series to be my introduction, but after going through the previous games, it makes sense that I wasn’t all that confused story-wise because it’s kind of secondary. The gameplay is where it’s at, while giving us a vibe of these characters. I will say that I never played the campaign on single-player mode, as I always wanted to play the campaign online, as I felt the second player was going to be better than the AI partner I’m with. So it was an interesting experience in itself, but with me on the easiest difficulty, it really helped me give my full thoughts, as I thought it was the most fun Resident Evil game I’ve played.

The gameplay is just so entertaining to play that even though it got rid of the horror aspects and replaced them with action, it still kept it grounded for the most part, that does keep the inventory limited. The AI in this is surprisingly really good. It's very helpful to me when it comes to the easiest difficulty, which I know whenever it comes to the hardest difficulty, that partner is going to become useless and a burden. The graphics still looked pretty good to this day. They had that gritty look to it, and I did like the characters of Chris and Sheva, who have decent chemistry as partners. Weaker is just fun as a villain that is a cartoon, but an entertaining one where he just wants to bring the outbreak to the world. The story is secondary once again, but I still like where the story is going, while bringing back Jill Valentine, who CLEARLY looks completely different from what I usually saw of her in the previous games.

I will say that with the game being very action-oriented, there do come some moments of QuickTime events that I don’t necessarily care about, and it does get a bit too over the top, which feels like something out of Fast & Furious instead of a zombie game. So it can get a bit too stupid at times, and the control scheme was something to get used to in the beginning. But Resident Evil 5 is still a lot of fun, so I can never get sick and tired of that one.

8/10

Short Review: Solid/Fun Action game but a meh resident evil game. Like no puzzles expect for line one section of the game. Not that hard of a game even on veteran. Gun upgrades feel good especially when maxed out. Story and the characters were the best part of the game. Wish sheva got more depth tho. Overall a solid action game to chill out too. 9/10

While Resident Evil 5 is definitely one of the weaker entries in the series it still manages to be fun in different ways if you do not go into this game expecting standards built from its predecessors and follow-ups.

The action and combat are good, guns feel satisfying to shoot and upgrade, collectibles are fun to get and while the story is not that thrilling all of it still achieves enough of RE's goofy camp horror that makes it a fun time. For example Irving's performance is so ridiculous and over the top I always get a laugh out of his on-screen appearances. That and how serious Chris is all the time makes for a really entertaining experience as he passively ignores the insanity of what occurs around him as he focuses purely on completing his goal. Also he literally punches a boulder out of his way - incredible.

The biggest difference to other RE games is the fact that a mandatory co-op partner (human or AI) takes away the atmosphere you'd normally get from most other RE games. Also, this game forgoes back-tracking and tight puzzling meaning it's much more linear and more in line with other 3rd person campaign shooters of the era.

The game overall feels a bit underbaked in terms of gameplay and experience but some things in Resident Evil never change. The feeling of having your back against the wall and having to make strategic decisions of how to use your last few rounds of ammo or consumables in order to survive waves of enemies that surround you is still great. The familiar stages of infection, ubiquity of headshots, bulbous weak spots on creatures all remind you you're still playing an RE game.

I'm in the middle of playing RE9 right now but I've been taking a break while not at my PC to enjoy the achievement hunting for this game on the steam deck and it's been surprising how much fun it is to fire up this game and complete a chapter or two on the go. This game won't change your life or anything but if you have an appetite for more RE and a friend to casually enjoy it with this game is still totally worth buying at a discounted price. (The deluxe edition of course so you can play the DLC missions too.)
The writing is horrid and the gameplay is clunky to hell and back, but I guess I can at least say that I've played it.

The best game in the world! It's Resident Evil 4 but on steroids.  The story and the gameplay are insane. The replayability value is tremendously great, you never get bored of it. This is Top-Tier Material. There are so many contents and things to discover and unlock in the game, you can never have enough. You always wanna replay the game, there are other badass Campaigns, DLC, contents and Mods aside from the Main Campagin and the Co-op is literally one of the best in the world if not the best, honestly i'd be surprised if this game isn't the best Co-op game in the world. It's a billion times better than modern games. It's also insane how the graphics and everything hold up so well to this day compared to the new gen games. The story is breath-taking. Everything is so well-made and has so much soul. The badass characters, the voice acting, the animations are Top-Notch. It's such an intense and priceless experience playing this with your friends. I can't imagine how badass the Remake is gonna be! I'd give it 100/10 if i could.
